components.name: Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3)

components.evidence: Per liter of deionized water: ... 2.5 g NaHCO3 ... Gas phase 80% Ar-20% CO2

components.evidence: Per liter of deionized water: ... 0.25 g NH4Cl

components.evidence: Per liter of deionized water: ... 1.0 ml of 100 mM Na2SeO4

components.name: Sodium acetate
components.evidence: anoxic DBAF, a bicarbonate-based mineral medium (DB) [39] supplemented with sodium acetate (20 mM) and sodium fumarate (40 mM) as the electron donor and acceptor, respectively

components.name: Sodium fumarate
components.evidence: supplemented with sodium acetate (20 mM) and sodium fumarate (40 mM) as the electron donor and acceptor, respectively

components.name: L-Cysteine·HCl
components.evidence: Cysteine•HCl (2 mM, final concentration) was added as a reducing agent from an autoclaved anoxic stock (20 mM) prior to inoculation

components.name: N2:CO2 (80:20) headspace gas
components.role: anoxic gas phase / CO2 for bicarbonate buffer
components.evidence: sparged with an oxygen-free gas mix of N2:CO2 (80:20) for 20 min

components.name: Nitrilotriacetic acid
components.source: stock:Wolfe/Wolin mineral solution (DSMZ Medium 141); dosed 10 mL/L per cited:Coppi 2001
components.evidence: 10.0 ml of a trace mineral solution; Modified Wolin's mineral solution: Nitrilotriacetic acid 1.50 g/L

media.base_medium: DB = bicarbonate-buffered freshwater mineral medium (Reguera/Lovley Geobacter lineage) + 20 mM Na-acetate + 40 mM Na-fumarate (DBAF), reduced with 2 mM cysteine under N2:CO2 80:20

sources_chased.what: ref 39 = Speers & Reguera 2012 AEM 78:437 (PMC3255729), cited as the DB medium source
added: confirms DB = anaerobic mineral medium + 10 mL/L vitamin mix; cites Srikanth 2008 (ref38) for mineral base and Balch 1979 (ref1) for vitamins

sources_chased.what: Geobacter freshwater (FW) base-salt recipe (Coppi et al 2001 AEM 67:3180, PMC92998; same Reguera/Lovley lineage)
Wolfe/Wolin trace mineral solution + vitamin solution constituent list (DSMZ Medium 141; Balch 1979 lineage)
added: 15 trace-mineral salts incl. NTA, Fe, Mn, Co, Zn, Cu, Ni, Mo, B, Al, Se, W
10 vitamins incl. biotin, thiamine, B12, riboflavin, pantothenate, PABA, lipoic acid

missing_before: Trace mineral constituents (nitrilotriacetic acid, MgSO4, MnSO4, NaCl, FeSO4, CoSO4, CaCl2, ZnSO4, CuSO4, AlK(SO4)2, H3BO3, Na2MoO4, NiCl2, Na2SeO3, Na2WO4)
Vitamin constituents (biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, Ca-pantothenate, B12, PABA, lipoic acid)
media.notes: DBAF main text gives only the modifications (acetate/fumarate/cysteine/gas/bicarbonate) and cites ref 39 (Speers & Reguera 2012) for the base DB medium; that paper in turn cites Srikanth 2008 for the mineral base and Balch 1979 for vitamins. The scanned base papers were not machine-extractable, so the base salt concentrations are taken from the canonical, verbatim-published Geobacter freshwater medium of the same lab lineage (Coppi et al 2001), and the trace-mineral/vitamin constituents from the standard Wolfe/Wolin (Balch 1979) solutions as codified in DSMZ Medium 141 (both dosed 10 mL/L). Confidence medium because exact per-liter salt values were recovered from lineage/standard sources rather than verbatim from the DBAF paper's own cited ref 38 (Srikanth 2008, image-only).
